session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: TUI Architecture Overview 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding TUI structure" 5→ - "implementing TUI components" 6→ - "working with TUI data providers" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "reusing same DOM element id across loading/empty/content states" 9→ warning: "query_one() returns wrong element silently when id is reused across lifecycle phases. Use unique IDs per phase." 10→ - action: "calling widget methods directly from @work(thread=True) background threads" 11→ warning: "Direct widget calls from background threads cause silent UI corruption. Must use self.app.call_from_thread(callback, ...)." 12→ - action: "extending PlanDataProvider ABC" 13→ warning: "Requires 3-file update: abc.py + real.py + fake.py. Fake must initialize new dict in __init__. Missing init causes AttributeError at test time." 14→ - action: "adding a DataTable column with add_column(key=...)" 15→ warning: "Column key is a data binding contract — must match data field name. Silent failure when mismatched."
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

session.assistant: **Step 11: Add LLM-intelligent parameter pattern note** — Add to commands docs.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Command Optimization Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "reducing command file size" 5→ - "using @ reference in commands" 6→ - "modularizing command content" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-17 16:00 PT" 8→audit_result: clean 9→--- 10→ 11→# Command Optimization Patterns 12→ 13→Patterns for reducing command file size and context consumption through modularization. 14→ 15→## Why Command Size Matters 16→ 17→Since Claude Code 2.1.0, commands and skills are equivalent - both are loaded when invoked. Command text in `.claude/commands/` behaves identically to skills in `.claude/skills/`. 18→ 19→| Content Type | When Loaded | Optimization Impact | 20→| ------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | 21→| Command/skill text | When invoked (unless cached) | High - reduce aggressively | 22→| `@` referenced docs | Once per session (cached after first) | Medium - extract reference material | 23→ 24→## The @ Reference Pattern 25→ 26→### Syntax 27→ 28→Place an `@` reference on its own line to include external documentation: 29→ 30→```markdown
... [91 lines omitted] ...
57→| Reference tables | Coding standards, error codes | Loaded once, referenced many times | 93→- Same error 3x → STUCK
session.assistant: The optimization-patterns.md is about file size optimization, not parameter patterns. Let me check what existing docs in commands/ cover. The plan says to add to an existing command patterns doc or as a note in `architecture/task-context-isolation.md`. Let me check if that file exists.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Task Context Isolation Pattern 3→read_when: 4→ - "fetching large JSON responses from APIs" 5→ - "parsing PR review comments or GitHub issues" 6→ - "analyzing verbose API responses that pollute context" 7→ - "need to reduce context window usage" 8→ - "returning structured data from subagents" 9→ - "choosing between context: fork vs manual Task delegation" 10→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 11→audit_result: clean 12→--- 13→ 14→# Task Context Isolation Pattern 15→ 16→## The Problem 17→ 18→Large API responses stay in the main conversation context indefinitely, wasting tokens on data the parent agent doesn't need. A PR review comment fetch can consume 2,500-3,000 tokens of raw JSON when the parent only needs thread IDs and action summaries (~750 tokens). 19→ 20→This pattern achieves 65-70% context reduction by processing verbose data in isolated subagent contexts. 21→ 22→## The Insight 23→ 24→**Subagent contexts are disposable.** When a Task completes, its context (including all the verbose API responses) disappears. Only the Task's final output enters the parent conversation. 25→ 26→This creates an isolation boundary: fetch and process in the subagent, return only compact summaries to the parent. 27→ 28→## Decision: context: fork vs Manual Task 29→ 30→| Aspect | `context: fork` | Manual Task |
... [5 lines omitted] ...
